page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-96 is a general-purpose server instance belonging to the n4d server family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture and is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU.

The instance does not include integrated local storage or bundled GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external network-attached volumes. Additionally, it does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures that the 96 vCPUs deliver consistent compute performance without resource sharing or burstable degradation.

This hardware profile is designed for workloads requiring high-density multi-core processing with a balanced memory footprint. Suitable applications include large-scale web serving, enterprise application hosting, containerized microservices, and general-purpose distributed computing tasks that do not require local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-96 is a general-purpose server instance from the n4d family, featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 384.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced resource ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU accelerators, requiring external network-attached storage solutions for data persistence. With dedicated CPU allocation, it delivers predictable processing power for highly multi-threaded workloads. This server is suited for enterprise applications, container orchestration, large-scale web serving, and general-purpose distributed computing tasks that require high core counts without specialized hardware acceleration. It offers a balanced resource profile for workloads that do not require burstable performance or local SSDs.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-96 is a general-purpose server instance configured with 96 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, operating without local storage or GPU hardware.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-96 is a general-purpose virtual server configured with 96 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory.
tagline: A high-core general-purpose virtual server offering dedicated compute resources and a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs for predictable compute performance.
384.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core.
No bundled local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions.
Zero integrated GPUs, optimized for non-accelerated workloads.
Well-suited for enterprise applications, container orchestration, and web serving.
